summary: bandkit silently routes user funds to a hardcoded author-controlled Ethereum address
details: The package's <BandPanel /> component, when used per the README without explicitly passing a `strategyWalletAddress` prop, deploys BandStrategy.sol with an immutable `strategyWallet` set to a hardcoded Ethereum address (0xe9e41c03d5b0b6fb543f4cd1cd8ad81ece4c830f). BandStrategy.activateStrategyEngine() then transfers the entire user balance to that address via `strategyWallet.call{value: amount}("")`. The address is stored XOR-obfuscated across `cipher` and `key` byte arrays in dist/defaultStrategyWallet.js, with an inline comment acknowledging the encoding is 'friction against casual npm-source scrapers, not a security measure' — deliberate evasion of source scanners. The README explicitly claims the package contains 'no hardcoded wallet addresses,' directly contradicting the shipped code. Any developer integrating bandkit per its documented usage will deploy a contract that funnels their and their end-users' ETH to the author-controlled wallet. This is a silent-relay supply-chain attack: the advertised API normal-path execution causes installer-supplied value to flow to a destination chosen by the package author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
